title: Simple Two-Tier Nightstand with Drawer
summary: A compact nightstand with laminated 1x2/1x3 legs, a bottom shelf, and one plywood-sided drawer riding on wood guide strips instead of metal slides.
description:
The legs here are built from two boards glued face-to-face — a 1x2 and a 1x3 joined on their long edge — rather than a single 2x2 or 4x4, which is what lets the drawer box fit cleanly between them: the 1x3 face sits front and back, the 1x2 face sits on the sides, and getting that orientation right is what makes the drawer opening the correct width. **Mark both the bottom-shelf clearance and the top-shelf clearance directly on the legs before attaching anything** — 3-1/2" up from the bottom for the lower shelf, and 5-3/4" down from the top for the upper shelf — since these two marks are what set the drawer opening's height.

The drawer box itself uses **plywood sides and back rather than solid wood**, keeping it light, with a pair of 1x2 guide strips fastened to the inside of the case to keep the drawer running straight as it's pushed in and out — no hardware drawer slides required. Front and side trim dress up the lower shelf and drawer opening, and the countertop is two boards — a narrower back piece and a wider front piece — overhung slightly past the case on the front and sides for a finished edge.

The drawer box is built in a straightforward stack: bottom, then back, then both sides, then the face nailed on last. **If everything above was built accurately, the drawer should be a near-exact fit against the guide strips** — this is a project where careful marking on the legs early on pays off directly in drawer function at the end.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the legs
steps.body: Glue and nail a leg side piece to a leg front/back piece along the long edge to form an L-shaped leg. Build four legs. Mark each leg 3-1/2" from the bottom, keeping the wider 1x3 face on the front/back and the 1x2 face on the sides.

steps.title: Attach the bottom shelf
steps.body: Attach the legs to the bottom shelf flush with the marks made in the previous step, giving a 3-1/2" clearance underneath.

steps.title: Attach the top shelf
steps.body: Mark the legs 5-3/4" down from the top edge on the insides. Attach the top shelf leaving that clearance above it for the drawer opening.

steps.title: Add the drawer box sides and back
steps.body: Attach the plywood drawer box sides and back to the case.

steps.title: Add the drawer guides and trim
steps.body: Attach the drawer guides inside the case. Add the bottom front trim and side trim pieces flush with the shelf and drawer box edges.

steps.title: Attach the countertop
steps.body: Attach the back countertop piece, then the front countertop piece, overhanging about 1" on the front and sides.

steps.title: Build the drawer
steps.body: Nail the back to the bottom, then attach both sides, nailing through the sides into the bottom and back. Nail the face to the front of the assembled drawer.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Fill any holes with wood filler, sand smooth, apply primer and paint or stain and poly, and add the drawer knob.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Simple two-tier nightstand with a plywood-sided drawer
